changelog: Adds three SD card failure mitigations, all live-tested on a real device before release. (1) The DISCARD-disable udev rule is now baked directly into reset.sh's golden-image-prep step, closing the gap where the rule only took effect starting a card's SECOND boot -- first_boot.sh does its heaviest writing during exactly the unprotected first boot, which is the likely root cause of the 2026-07-06 corruption incident. (2) run_flash_canary(): a fresh device's very first boot now does a 64MB write/readback verification (dd with conv=fsync, sha256 compared) before any other provisioning writes happen; a failure aborts provisioning outright rather than shipping a bad card. (3) check_config_integrity(): every maintenance-pass boot on an already-provisioned device now verifies configuration.yaml still parses as YAML and every .storage/* file still parses as JSON -- the same failure signature both 2026 corruption incidents produced -- and raises a persistent_notification (new sensor.cytech_integrity_alert + automation in packages/cytech.yaml) the moment something doesn't, instead of the first sign being HA's own recovery mode with no usable backup. Also: ensure_ssh_admin_access now authorizes a second, fixed operator SSH key (CYTECH_OPERATOR_PUBKEY, alongside each device's own internal key) so the operator can pull backups/administer any device without needing each device's random per-device password.
warnings: HA will restart automatically after this update (packages/cytech.yaml changed).

Devices with a genuinely stale/corrupted .storage or configuration.yaml file will start showing a new 'data integrity issue' notification on this update -- this is the new check working as intended, not a regression caused by the update itself.
The offsite-backup SSH key (CYTECH_OPERATOR_PUBKEY) only takes effect if that secret is set in .cytech_secrets -- devices without it are unaffected by that part of this release.
